channel.title: Comments for page "AFTERLIFE OPHIDIAN /// The Foundation Over Heaven"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "AFTERLIFE OPHIDIAN /// The Foundation Over Heaven"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. The whole project and Nagiros' works are like candy for someone like me who is still balls deep in deprussy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I'm late to the party, but this is very good. I know I missed like half of it, but even the part I did understand warranted an upvote. +1, and very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This resonates with me in a way I can't clearly explain.
Amazing stuff. +1

This tale strikes a pretty resonant chord with me- I love the whimsical, fantastical way this authoritarian government is presented. It makes it feel like a demented Tim Burton movie, and the references to other SCPs (and JoJo!) absolutely tie it together.

In regards to the author post, I'm currently going through something similar. I'm aware this was released a year ago, but I hope you've found your peace since.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting idea and well-written, +1 from me

Good luck on finding some friends, hope things improve!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Hey, thanks, I'm fine. Sorry if that wasn't well-communicated by my author post.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: Overreaction (if slightly warranted). Talk to me or Dex for details.

Good luck Nagiros.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Accompanying Music — Sing Me To Sleep by Matstubs

This was a very personal tale to write, especially after a few months of forced hiatus. I hope you all like it. I've never done anything in this style before, and a big thank you goes to Kybard for refining it with a draft critique I desperately needed.

A companion piece (and refutation) may be in the works as well. Thanks for reading! ✨🦀✨

+ Image Sources
- Image Sources
  • Filename "breaker" was created by me from this asset, with reference to this art by SunnyClockwork from F̶o̶u̶r̶t̶e̶e̶n̶ Thirteen by Fantem.
  • The Three Arrows pagebreak is public domain and sourced from Wikipedia.
+ A Note on References
- A Note on References

Consider the following to be an incomplete list. I may have subconsciously drawn from other sources; for example, the "moon no longer rose" line may be a reference to "Black Moon Howling" or S. D. Locke's Proposal, but I'm not sure.

The Foundation Over Heaven

A reference to Heaven Ascension Dio's Stand from JoJo's Bizarre Adventure: Eyes of Heaven, named The World Over Heaven. The power of Dio's original Stand, The World, leads Dio to proclaim himself king over Earth and time. The World Over Heaven is its evolved form.

The serpent's eyes pierce through her darkened lair.

This is Anantashesha, from SCP-3000 by djkaktus.

Their numbers flowed over plains and hills, led a shadowy colossus — the voice of The Administrator emanated from his lips.

The Administrator's design is evocative of my own SCP-4260, although he is not strictly SCP-4260 in this tale.

He was a man brought to the brink of the ultimate, striding unimpeded over mountains and trampling ancient land under the gravity of his personhood.

Hmm.

The Administrator's insignia shone bright against the crimson sky …

Suggests the Scarlet King and apocalypse.

like a policeman's badge: three inward arrows and a ring, branded onto moving flesh before his ascension.

Suggests the Three Arrows symbol, although The Administrator's brand is the standard Foundation logo. I find it interesting that the two are so stylistically similar. When the Foundation logo was first suggested by far2, I recall is being likened to a police badge.

As gunfire engulfed the town, her fingerprint remained on the broken glass of that old window.

…

Giants were in motion between the corpses of skyscrapers, silently heralding a new Earth.

References to memorable lines of Misery Makes Company by Tiefling. I really like that tale.

My body floats forever, the perfect anti-Talloran.

Alludes to SCP-3999 by LordStonefish, which I posit as SCP-3000's opposite.

Between cities and mountains walked everything ancient man had feared and hated. For a year, everything happened at once.

This is inspired by the opening of Mud on the Carpet by Djoric.

Overhead, a mechanical giant loomed and wandered, glass eyes observing a glass world.

Suggests the Broken God from the TwistedGears-Kaktus Proposal by TwistedGears and djkaktus.

"No, we can't ess-sea-pea that thing," said another.

Reference to how DrChandra's Herman Fuller characters talk about the Foundation, particularly in SCP-3717. It's only purpose here is to imply some sort of informational-breakdown.

Three inward shots rang out as I spiraled off the cliff edge.

Evokes the arrows from the Foundation logo.

"O World, O peoples, O Mankind: … May this foundation bring peace to Mankind."

This language is very similar to the Doctors of the Church Hub. All other connections are accidental.

Hear now our words of love to you, mere utterances from the dark.

We die in the dark, and all that.

traitors were shot behind the chemical shed.

This is a reference to V for Vendetta, which I hate. A character named Evey Hammond is tortured and threatened with execution "behind the chemical shed". The graphic novel is noted for its praise of anarchist philosophy. Interestingly, no executions behind chemical sheds occur.
